channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: You can make a multicolor MacBook Neo out of Apple’s spare parts
item.description: If you're having trouble deciding on a color for a new MacBook Neo, you might not have to choose just one. The folks over at MacWorld found that Apple doesn't appear to limit the colors of spare...

item.title: My cache fix was fine—until it wasn’t
item.description: My cache fix was fine until it wasn't. The day I realized we were shipping amnesia My cache fix passed tests, code review, and staging. It still caused production pain two weeks later, for a...

item.title: Sometimes your AI server is down and you remember you actually do know how to code by hand just fine.
ISO 27001 Internal Audit Checklist for Small Teams
item.description: ISO 27001 internal audits often get treated like a mini certification audit. That is usually where the pain starts. Small teams already have enough on their plate. They are running BAU support,...

item.title: ¿Por qué tu z-index: 9999 no funciona? Entendiendo el Contexto de Apilamiento
item.description: ¿Alguna vez has puesto un z-index: 9999 a un elemento y, aun así, se queda tercamente detrás de otro que tiene un z-index: 1? 🤯 No es un bug del navegador. Es simplemente que has entrado en un...

item.title: From LLMs to AI Agents: A Practical Guide to Automation with LangChain, Zapier, and n8n
item.description: Introduction: The Automation Gold Rush Every developer right now is hearing the same buzzwords: AI, agents, automation, LLMs. Your LinkedIn feed is flooded with it. Startup pitches are built on...

item.title: One honojs file for entire web scraping API

item.title: High-Performance Video Streaming API in Go
item.description: Why Go for a Video API When DailyWatch needed an API that could handle thousands of concurrent requests with minimal latency, Go was a natural fit. Its standard library includes a production-grade...

item.title: Billing That Won’t Break: Deterministic Design to Kill Double Charges (API Contract DB Boundary Execution Patterns)
item.description: Billing isn’t protected by “correct code” alone. What protects it is determinism under execution wobble: retries timeouts duplicate requests (user double-clicks) async delays worker crashes...

item.title: I Built a Portable SIEM Toolkit That Runs Sigma Rules Without Deploying a Full SIEM
item.description: You don't always have a full SIEM stack at your fingertips. Maybe you're a student spinning up a home lab, a junior SOC analyst triaging logs on a budget, or a pentester who just needs to check...

item.title: “Skills” in Claude Aren’t About Prompts — They’re About Context Design
item.description: Introduction When I first came across “Skills” in Claude Code, it looked like just another abstraction over prompts. But after spending some time with it, the interesting part wasn’t what skills...

item.title: On Anthropic’s Mythos Preview and Project Glasswing
item.description: The cybersecurity industry is obsessing over Anthropic’s new model, Claude Mythos Preview, and its effects on cybersecurity. Anthropic said that it is not releasing it to the general public...

item.title: Read OpenAI’s latest internal memo about beating the competition — including Anthropic
item.description: OpenAI's chief revenue officer, Denise Dresser, sent a four-page memo to employees on Sunday about the company's strategic direction, emphasizing the need to lock in users and grow its enterprise...

item.title: Meta Is Warned That Facial Recognition Glasses Will Arm Sexual Predators
item.description: More than 70 organizations, including the ACLU, EPIC, and Fight for the Future, say the AI smart glasses feature would endanger abuse victims, immigrants, and LGBTQ+ people.

item.title: Why opinion on AI is so divided
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. In an industry that doesn’t stand still, Stanford’s AI...

item.title: Slate raises $650 million to make its budget electric truck
item.description: Slate Auto, the EV startup backed by Jeff Bezos, raised $650 million to fund its effort to build an affordable electric pickup truck expected to start in the mid-$20,000s. The company plans on...

item.title: Microsoft is testing OpenClaw-like AI bots for 365 Copilot
item.description: Microsoft is looking into ways it can integrate OpenClaw-style features into 365 Copilot, according to a report from The Information. The test reportedly comes as part of efforts to make its 365...

item.title: Google’s Pixel 10A is a good midrange phone that’s $50 off
item.description: If it’s time for you to upgrade your phone, it’s worth considering the Pixel 10A, Google’s latest midrange phone that has some (but not all) of the new software features of the pricier $599 Pixel...

item.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch signals IPO readiness as AI agents fuel revenue surge
item.description: "The company is ready and getting more ready for every day," Rauch said about an IPO at HumanX conference.

item.title: Pragmata is just OK, but it could’ve been great
item.description: Over the last few years, Capcom has done an exceptional job of solidifying its lineup of big, long-running franchises. The likes of Monster Hunter, Resident Evil, and Street Fighter have all been...

item.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
item.description: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), in partnership with the Indonesian National Police, has dismantled the infrastructure associated with a global phishing operation that leveraged an...

item.title: SwitchBot’s button-pressing robot is now available with a rechargeable battery
item.description: SwitchBot is introducing a new version of the tiny button-pressing robot that helped launch the company a decade ago. The functionality of the new SwitchBot Bot is exactly the same as the original...

item.title: Can Puck reinvent the news business for the influencer age?
item.description: Today, I’m talking with Sarah Personette, CEO of Puck, which is a fancy new media company that’s been around for just about five years now. Puck hires big stars and gives them newsletters that are...

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg is reportedly building an AI clone to replace him in meetings
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg could soon have an AI clone of himself to interact with and provide feedback to employees, according to a report from the Financial Times. Sources tell the outlet that...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Fiber Optic Spying, Windows Rootkit, AI Vulnerability Hunting and More
item.description: Monday is back, and the weekend’s backlog of chaos is officially hitting the fan. We are tracking a critical zero-day that has been quietly living in your PDFs for months, plus some aggressive...

item.title: Want to understand the current state of AI? Check out these charts.
item.description: If you’re following AI news, you’re probably getting whiplash. AI is a gold rush. AI is a bubble. AI is taking your job. AI can’t even read a clock. The 2026 AI Index from Stanford University’s...

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz blockade is causing a slow-moving food crisis
item.description: Farmers are very busy in the spring, under pressure to get crops into the ground just as the Northern Hemisphere begins to thaw. But this year has been different for many, thanks in large part to...

item.title: The Download: how humans make decisions, and Moderna’s “vaccine” word games
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. You have no choice in reading this article—maybe How do...

item.title: Your MTTD Looks Great. Your Post-Alert Gap Doesn't
item.description: Anthropic restricted its Mythos Preview model last week after it autonomously found and exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in every major operating system and browser. Palo Alto Networks' Wendi...

item.title: The Internet's Most Powerful Archiving Tool Is in Peril
item.description: As major news outlets cut off the Wayback Machine, journalists and advocacy groups are rallying to protect the Internet Archive’s vast collection of web pages.
